message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-d8qdy (algebraic_connectivity directed)

Fixed in commit 9a810dc. algebraic_connectivity now raises
NetworkXNotImplemented('not implemented for directed type') on
directed input, mirroring nx's @not_implemented_for decorator.
Sister spectral functions were already correct — algebraic_connectivity
was the last outlier in this class of defects.

Sweep: 6718 passed, 0 failed (+6 new regression tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -54,7 +54,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-bvf5w","title":"draw_bipartite should accept top_nodes as positional arg matching test expectations","description":"fnx.draw_bipartite has signature (G, **kwargs) where top_nodes must be passed as kwarg. But three tests in test_drawing_extras.py call it positionally as draw_bipartite(graph, top_nodes, ...).

Pre-existing test failures:
- test_draw_bipartite_uses_bipartite_layout_when_pos_missing
- test_draw_bipartite_transformed_graph_uses_local_layout
- test_draw_bipartite_transformed_graph_respects_explicit_pos

The tests reflect the project's intent: fnx's draw_bipartite should accept top_nodes as the second positional argument (a friendly extension of nx's API since nx itself doesn't take top_nodes). Without it, the user has to compute bipartite_sets explicitly which the test explicitly avoids.

Fix: extend signature to (G, top_nodes=None, **kwargs).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:03:41.278034803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:06:29.357628656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:06:29.355658940Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-cirha","title":"parity: 34 top-level functions accept positional args that nx made keyword-only","description":"fnx's signatures use POSITIONAL_OR_KEYWORD for params nx declares as KEYWORD_ONLY (after *). Affected: dfs_edges/dfs_tree/dfs_predecessors/dfs_successors/dfs_postorder_nodes/dfs_preorder_nodes (sort_neighbors), random_spanning_tree/random_labeled_tree/random_unlabeled_tree/random_labeled_rooted_tree/random_unlabeled_rooted_tree/random_unlabeled_rooted_forest (seed, multiplicative, number_of_*), connected_watts_strogatz_graph/dense_gnm_random_graph/duplication_divergence_graph/gnm_random_graph/random_lobster_graph/random_powerlaw_tree/generalized_petersen_graph (create_using), directed_edge_swap (nswap, max_tries, seed), from_numpy_array (nodelist), harmonic_diameter (weight), identified_nodes (store_contraction_as), number_of_spanning_trees (weight, root), subgraph_view (filter_node, filter_edge). Forward-compat issue: code using positional form on fnx breaks when ported to nx. Fix: insert * before the documented keyword-only params in each wrapper.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:36:02.353496302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:40:25.924391077Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:40:25.922533471Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 863a001. 25 functions now match nx's POSITIONAL_OR_KEYWORD vs KEYWORD_ONLY param kinds. Code using positional form for these params now correctly raises TypeError on both fnx and nx, preventing forward-compat foot-guns. number_of_spanning_trees got a Python wrapper around the Rust binding. 31 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-cujmq","title":"parity: VF2 GraphMatcher/DiGraphMatcher/MultiGraphMatcher classes missing","description":"networkx.algorithms.isomorphism exposes GraphMatcher, DiGraphMatcher, MultiGraphMatcher, MultiDiGraphMatcher classes for the classical VF2 algorithm with rich API: subgraph_is_isomorphic(), subgraph_isomorphisms_iter(), match(), candidate_pairs_iter(), semantic_feasibility(), syntactic_feasibility(), initialize(). fnx exposes only the top-level functions (is_isomorphic, vf2pp_*) but no Matcher classes. Add wrapper classes that delegate to nx.algorithms.isomorphism via _networkx_graph_for_parity, exporting as fnx.GraphMatcher etc.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-networkx","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:30:43.669346473Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:18:29.924704555Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T09:18:29.922845114Z","close_reason":"Completed in 742c813","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-d8qdy","title":"algebraic_connectivity silently returns value on directed graph; nx raises NetworkXNotImplemented","description":"fnx.algebraic_connectivity silently computes a value on directed graphs. nx is decorated with @not_implemented_for('directed') and raises NetworkXNotImplemented.

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.DiGraph([(1,2),(2,1)])
  >>> fnx.algebraic_connectivity(g)
  2.0
  >>> nx.algebraic_connectivity(nx.DiGraph([(1,2),(2,1)]))
  NetworkXNotImplemented: not implemented for directed type

Sister functions fiedler_vector (already raises on directed via the Rust path) and spectral_ordering (correctly accepts both per nx) handle directed input correctly. algebraic_connectivity was the remaining outlier — it computes the Fiedler value via laplacian_spectrum, which works on directed graphs but produces a meaningless number for the undirected Laplacian interpretation.

Fix: add if G.is_directed(): raise NetworkXNotImplemented('not implemented for directed type') at the top of fnx.algebraic_connectivity, mirroring nx's @not_implemented_for decorator.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T07:24:17.290056906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T07:24:48.721885471Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-d8qdy","title":"algebraic_connectivity silently returns value on directed graph; nx raises NetworkXNotImplemented","description":"fnx.algebraic_connectivity silently computes a value on directed graphs. nx is decorated with @not_implemented_for('directed') and raises NetworkXNotImplemented.

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.DiGraph([(1,2),(2,1)])
  >>> fnx.algebraic_connectivity(g)
  2.0
  >>> nx.algebraic_connectivity(nx.DiGraph([(1,2),(2,1)]))
  NetworkXNotImplemented: not implemented for directed type

Sister functions fiedler_vector (already raises on directed via the Rust path) and spectral_ordering (correctly accepts both per nx) handle directed input correctly. algebraic_connectivity was the remaining outlier — it computes the Fiedler value via laplacian_spectrum, which works on directed graphs but produces a meaningless number for the undirected Laplacian interpretation.

Fix: add if G.is_directed(): raise NetworkXNotImplemented('not implemented for directed type') at the top of fnx.algebraic_connectivity, mirroring nx's @not_implemented_for decorator.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T07:24:17.290056906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T07:29:25.398277537Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T07:29:25.395450805Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-d967f","title":"parity: approximation treewidth/steiner/dominating/max-cut/ramsey/densest wrappers missing (10 functions)","description":"After the TSP family (br-8vobd), 10 more approximation algorithms remain unexposed: treewidth_min_degree(G), treewidth_min_fill_in(G), steiner_tree(G, terminal_nodes, weight, method), min_edge_dominating_set(G), min_maximal_matching(G), min_weighted_dominating_set(G, weight), one_exchange(G, initial_cut, seed, weight), randomized_partitioning(G, seed, p, weight), ramsey_R2(G), densest_subgraph(G, iterations, method). All are pure heuristics with no Rust counterpart — add wrappers via the existing _call_networkx_approximation_for_parity helper, export at top-level, add to __all__ under the approximation section.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T19:34:18.988742298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T19:57:48.971397375Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T19:57:48.969471288Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 66ec413: 10 approximation wrappers (treewidth_min_degree/min_fill_in, steiner_tree, min_edge_dominating_set, min_maximal_matching, min_weighted_dominating_set, one_exchange, randomized_partitioning, ramsey_R2, densest_subgraph). All verified against nx on K5/C6/P6/K6/C7/BA20 graphs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-dc14n","title":"Graph.edges(nbunch) returns edges in wrong order vs nx","description":"fnx.Graph.edges(nbunch) (and .edges(node)) returns edges in a different iteration order than nx. The all-edges (no nbunch) case matches; only the filtered case drifts.

Repro:
  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','e'),('a','c')]  
  Both libs: adj['a'] = ['b','c']
  
  fnx.edges(['a','b']) -> [('b','c'), ('a','c'), ('a','b')]
  nx .edges(['a','b']) -> [('a','b'), ('a','c'), ('b','c')]
  
  fnx.edges('a') -> [('a','c'), ('a','b')]
  nx .edges('a') -> [('a','b'), ('a','c')]   (matches adj['a'] order)
  
  fnx.edges(['a','b','c']) -> [('c','d'), ('c','b'), ('c','a'), ('a','b')]
  nx .edges(['a','b','c']) -> [('a','b'), ('a','c'), ('b','c'), ('c','d')]

nx's contract: iterate nbunch_list in user-given order, for each node yield edges (u, v) in adj[u] order, skipping edges where v is already-seen. fnx's Rust path appears to iterate G.nodes() (full graph) and filter by nbunch — completely different.

Drop-in code that iterated G.edges(nbunch) expecting nx's adj-ordered output broke. DiGraph cases (edges, in_edges, out_edges) match — only undirected edges() with nbunch drifts.

Fix: override EdgeDataView._materialize to iterate nbunch in user order with adj-iteration, mirroring nx's exact algorithm.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:42:44.701569209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:49:57.853658449Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T17:49:57.851586465Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-dg2dn","title":"core_number/k_core/k_shell/k_crust/k_corona accept self-loop graphs; nx raises NetworkXNotImplemented; eulerian_path drift on self-loops","description":"Two parity defects:

(1) core_number-family functions silently accept graphs with self-loops; nx raises NetworkXNotImplemented:

  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1,2),(2,3),(3,1),(1,1)])
  >>> fnx.core_number(g)        # silently returns {1: 2, 2: 2, 3: 2}
  >>> nx.core_number(nx.Graph(g))
  NetworkXNotImplemented: Input graph has self loops which is not permitted; Consider using G.remove_edges_from(nx.selfloop_edges(G)).

Affects core_number, k_core, k_shell, k_crust, k_corona — all share the _raw_core_number Rust binding which has no self-loop guard.

(2) eulerian_path raises 'Graph has no Eulerian paths' on graphs that ARE Eulerian (after the br-r37-c1-792dv has_eulerian_path/is_eulerian fix):

  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1,2),(2,3),(3,1),(1,1)])
  >>> fnx.has_eulerian_path(g)        # True (correct after br-r37-c1-792dv)
  >>> fnx.is_eulerian(g)               # True (correct)
  >>> list(fnx.eulerian_path(g))
  NetworkXError: Graph has no Eulerian paths.    # nx returns [(1,2),(2,3),(3,1),(1,1)]

The Rust _raw_eulerian_path mishandles self-loops the same way the corresponding boolean predicates did. Delegate to nx whenever the graph contains a self-loop.

Fix: add self-loop guard in core_number raising NetworkXNotImplemented with nx's wording. Add self-loop guard in eulerian_path delegating to nx.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T06:29:52.956609614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T06:36:03.395153162Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T06:36:03.392377045Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
